resposta:
solution: 
```class Solution:
    def minimumCost(self, s: str) -> int:
        n = len(s)
        cost = 0
        ones = 0
        for i in range(n):
            if s[i] == '1':
                ones += 1
            if i == n - 1 or s[i + 1] != s[i]:
                cost += min(i + 1 + ones, n - i)
                ones = 0
        return cost
```

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: This solution iterates over the string once, keeping track of the number of consecutive ones encountered. When a zero is found or the end of the string is reached, it calculates the minimum cost to flip all ones up to that point and adds it to the total cost. This approach has a high efficiency due to its linear time complexity and constant space complexity.
